summary: Clear sound‑board capture of a late‑1995 Portland show that many find musically uneven, with highlights like Peggy‑O and Black Peter but a generally under‑cooked performance.
review: The SBD source is crisp and well‑balanced, letting listeners hear the nuances of the band even when the playing feels sluggish.  Reviewers note several bright spots – Peggy‑O, Black Peter, Box of Rain and a strong Crazy Fingers stretch – but most lament a plodding tempo, distracted drums and a wan Jerry Garcia.  The show divides fans: some defend it as a worthwhile late‑era document, others deem it one of the weaker shows of ’95.

ai_rating.rationale: Recording quality is generally praised (good soundboard), but musical performance receives mixed to negative feedback, pulling the overall rating toward the middle of the scale.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Most reviewers call the SBD clear and crisp; no major drop‑outs are mentioned. A few note occasional wind or ambient noise, but overall the mix is balanced.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set drifts with uneven tempos – slower, plodding numbers in the first set and a safer, more predictable second set. Highlights break through the overall sluggishness.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Most reviewers describe Garcia as lacking gusto, playing sloppy solos and sounding exhausted; a few note he still managed decent moments on Peggy‑O.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned only in passing – seen looking over at Jerry, offering encouragement; no specific musical comments.
band_member_comments.Bob: Briefly noted as watching Jerry; no detailed critique of his guitar or vocals.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince (keyboards) is referenced during the “Watchtower” and space sections; otherwise keyboards receive little comment.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill/Mickey are called out for being conspicuous, distracting, and sometimes struggling to lock into slower tempos.
